question_text: A police officer with a siren drives towards a stationary person at high speed. Compared to when the car is stationary, the person observes that the siren sound has a higher frequency and shorter wavelength. Which of the following best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: The moving car produces sound waves with more energy, so the frequency increases.

options.label: B
options.text: The car compresses the sound waves as it moves towards the observer, reducing the wavelength and increasing the frequency heard.

options.label: C
options.text: The speed of sound increases when the car moves, causing the frequency to increase.

options.label: D
options.text: The siren's actual frequency increases when the car accelerates towards the observer.

explanation: This is the Doppler effect—when a sound source moves towards an observer, the sound waves are compressed (wavelength decreases) and more waves reach the observer per second (frequency increases), making the sound appear higher-pitched. The speed of sound and the siren's actual frequency remain unchanged.
